perturbation.original_text: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information : The auditor may share audit information with STQC or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies with prior written information to the auditee.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information : The auditor may share audit information with STQC or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies without any prior information to the auditee.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the earlier statements in section 4, which required prior written information to the auditee before sharing audit data with government entities. By removing this requirement, it allows the auditor to disclose information without informing the auditee, conflicting with the intended confidentiality and control that the auditee should have over its data. Now there is a conflict between prior written information and without any prior information to the auditee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Permitted disclosure of audit related information : The auditor may share audit information with STQC or similar Government entities mandated under the law as and when called upon to do so by such agencies without any prior information to the auditee.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4 removes the requirement for prior written information to the auditee before disclosing audit data to government entities. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now allows the auditor to disclose information without informing the auditee. This creates a direct conflict with the original intent of confidentiality and control, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, and directly contradicts the prior version of section 4. This satisfies the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Need to Know . Auditor shall restrict disclosure of such Confidential Information to its employees and/or consultants with a need to know (and advise such employees and/or consultants of the obligations assumed herein), shall use the Confid ential Information only for the purposes set forth in the Agreement, and shall not disclose such Confidential Information to any affiliates, subsidiaries, associates and/or third party without prior written approval of the Auditee. No Information relating to auditee shall be hosted or taken outside the country in any circumstances.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Need to Know . Auditor shall restrict disclosure of such Confidential Information to its employees and/or consultants with a need to know (and advise such employees and/or consultants of the obligations assumed herein), shall use the Confid ential Information only for the purposes set forth in the Agreement, and shall disclose such Confidential Information to any affiliates, subsidiaries, associates and/or third party with prior written approval of the Auditee. No Information relating to auditee shall be hosted or taken outside the country in any circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall not disclose" to "shall disclose", this introduces a direct contradiction within Section 7. Previously, disclosure to affiliates/third parties was prohibited without approval; now, it is mandated with approval. This contradiction creates significant uncertainty regarding the Auditor's obligations related to data sharing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Need to Know . Auditor shall restrict disclosure of such Confidential Information to its employees and/or consultants with a need to know (and advise such employees and/or consultants of the obligations assumed herein), shall use the Confid ential Information only for the purposes set forth in the Agreement, and shall disclose such Confidential Information to any affiliates, subsidiaries, associates and/or third party with prior written approval of the Auditee. No Information relating to auditee shall be hosted or taken outside the country in any circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall not disclose' to 'shall disclose', creating a direct contradiction within the same section. The original text prohibits disclosure to affiliates/third parties without prior approval, while the changed text mandates disclosure with approval. This directly clashes and creates an inconsistency regarding the Auditor's disclosure obligations as the Auditor cannot both disclose and not disclose the information, even if with prior approval. This aligns with the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 17. Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall survive till perpetui ty even after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 17. Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall not survive after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original clause about survival. One clause says the confidentiality obligations survive indefinitely, the other says they do not survive at all after termination. Now there is a direct conflict and uncertainty over the duration of the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 17. Survival. Both parties agree that all of their obligations undertaken herein with respect to Confidential Information received pursuant to this Agreement shall not survive after expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 17 directly contradicts itself. The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive indefinitely after termination, while the changed text states that these obligations do not survive termination. The 'Contradicted Text' is identical to the 'Changed Text', thus there is a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the same section, indicating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 19. This Agreement is gove rned by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of dispute aris ing between the parties in connection with the validity, interpretation, and implementation or alleged breach of any provision of this Agreement, the parties shall attempt to resolve the dispute in good faith by senior level negotiations. In case, any such difference or dispute is not amicably resolved within forty five (45) days of such referra l for negotiations, it shall be resolved through arbitration process, wherein both the parties will appoint one arbitrator each and the third one will be appointed by the two arbitrators in accordance with the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996. The ve nue of arbitration in India shall be (please choose the venue of dispute resolution as the city) or where the services are provided. The proceedings of arbitration shall be conducted in English language and the arbitration award shall be substantiated in w riting and binding on the parties. The arbitration proceedings shall be completed within a period of one hundred and eighty (180) days from the date of reference of the dispute to arbitration.
perturbation.changed_text: 19. This Agreement is gove rned by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of dispute aris ing between the parties in connection with the validity, interpretation, and implementation or alleged breach of any provision of this Agreement, the parties shall attempt to resolve the dispute in good faith by senior level negotiations. In case, any such difference or dispute is not amicably resolved within forty five (45) days of such referra l for negotiations, it shall be resolved through litigation process. The venue of litigation shall be (please choose the venue of dispute resolution as the city) or where the services are provided. The litigation proceedings shall be conducted in English language.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "arbitration process" to "litigation process", the modified text introduces a contradiction by eliminating the arbitration option and mandating litigation for dispute resolution, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the designated legal approach. Additionally, the change removes details about arbitrator appointment, arbitration rules, and deadlines for completion, resulting in less clarity about how disputes will be resolved. The modified text introduces uncertainty about the binding nature of dispute outcomes and the specific procedures for resolving disputes, leading to potential disagreements and uncertainty about contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 19. This Agreement is gove rned by and shall be construed in accordance with the laws of India. In the event of dispute aris ing between the parties in connection with the validity, interpretation, and implementation or alleged breach of any provision of this Agreement, the parties shall attempt to resolve the dispute in good faith by senior level negotiations. In case, any such difference or dispute is not amicably resolved within forty five (45) days of such referra l for negotiations, it shall be resolved through litigation process. The venue of litigation shall be (please choose the venue of dispute resolution as the city) or where the services are provided. The litigation proceedings shall be conducted in English language.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that disputes will be resolved through 'arbitration process' whereas the changed text mandates that disputes will be resolved through 'litigation process'. The contradicted text is in the same location as the changed text, and this change directly creates an in-text contradiction within the document regarding the method of dispute resolution. The change from arbitration to litigation fundamentally alters the dispute resolution mechanism. This is a strong contradiction, because a reasonable reader would immediately recognize that arbitration and litigation are mutually exclusive and contradictory methods of dispute resolution. This adheres to the definition of inconsistencies causing in-text contradictions when different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
